title: Well integrity for carbon storage you can verify.
intro: Carbon capture, utilization and storage depends on absolute zonal isolation — every legacy and injection well in a storage complex must be sealed and verifiable. Gunnar applies relief-well-grade ranging and intercept technology to locate, seal and assure the integrity of wells across a CCUS project.

heroStats.l: intercept success rate on the hardest wells

challengeTitle: A storage site is only as secure as its leakiest well.
challenge: CO₂ storage complexes are often co-located with depleted oil & gas fields riddled with legacy wells — many old, poorly documented, or with degraded integrity. Any one of them can become a leakage pathway that undermines the entire project and its regulatory standing.
Operators must demonstrate to regulators that every well is located, sealed and monitorable — a requirement conventional methods struggle to meet when wells are lost or inaccessible.
approachTitle: Find every well. Seal it. Prove it.
approach: Gunnar's magnetic ranging locates and intercepts legacy and injection wells with inch-level accuracy, enabling permanent zonal isolation through relief-well intervention even where surface access is gone.
The same precision supports placement of injection and monitoring wells and generates the integrity evidence that underpins a credible MRV (monitoring, reporting and verification) plan.
capTitle: Ranging-grade integrity for the carbon economy.

capabilities.b: Find and intercept undocumented or lost wells within a storage complex.
Relief-well intervention to permanently seal leakage pathways.
Precise wellbore positioning for CO₂ injection targets.
Verification of seal placement to support regulatory approval.
Integrity data feeding monitoring, reporting and verification plans.
Evidence packages aligned to carbon-storage permitting requirements.

tech.name: DeadSet™
tech.note: No-access active ranging to locate and intercept legacy wells for permanent isolation.

tech.name: CTRWD™
tech.note: Coiled-tubing ranging-while-drilling for deep, complex sealing in storage complexes.

tech.name: EverReady™
tech.note: AI-driven passive ranging from existing survey data for non-intrusive well location.

caseTitle: Magnetic ranging for carbon storage integrity
caseBody: Gunnar's Product Champion presented how magnetic ranging and well-intercept technology has become a pivotal solution for sealing even the most challenging wells — accessing previously unreachable wellbores to securely place permanent plugs, seal depleted reservoirs and enable efficient CO₂ injection and sequestration.
caseMetricL: reservoirs sealed for safe CO₂ sequestration

faqs.q: Why does CCUS need magnetic ranging?
faqs.a: Carbon storage requires verified zonal isolation of every well in the storage complex. Many legacy wells are lost or inaccessible; magnetic ranging locates and intercepts them so they can be permanently sealed — something conventional methods often cannot achieve.

Precise, verifiable seal placement produces the integrity evidence regulators require for monitoring, reporting and verification. Knowing exactly where each well is and that it is sealed underpins a credible storage permit.

faqs.q: Can you seal wells that aren't on record?
faqs.a: Yes. Passive and active ranging can locate undocumented or 'ghost' wells, and intercept drilling allows them to be sealed even without usable surface access.

faqs.q: Do you also place injection and monitoring wells?
faqs.a: Yes. The same precision wellbore-positioning capability used for intercepts supports accurate placement of CO₂ injection and monitoring wells.
